summary: Split the large assembly file asm/melee/mn/mndiagram.s into multiple assembly translation-unit-style pieces, adding asm/melee/mn/mndiagram2.s and asm/melee/mn/mndiagram3.s and registering them in obj_files.mk. The diff is mostly mechanical code movement: mndiagram.s loses roughly 4.5k lines while the new files receive the moved assembly. One local symbol at 8023F540 was promoted from lbl_8023F540 to global func_8023F540, likely to preserve cross-file references after the split. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided slice.

key_files.role: Original large assembly file; retained the earlier portion and removed a large later block that was moved into split files.
key_files.evidence: Changed by +4/-4493 lines; diff shows a large deletion beginning after lbl_802439E8.

key_files.role: New split assembly file containing part of the moved mndiagram assembly.
key_files.evidence: Added with 2640 lines in one hunk.

key_files.role: New split assembly file containing another part of the moved mndiagram assembly.
key_files.evidence: Added with 1898 lines in one hunk.

key_files.role: Build object list updated so the new split assembly files are assembled/linked.
key_files.evidence: Changed by +2 lines, consistent with adding mndiagram2 and mndiagram3 object entries.

what_changed: Moved thousands of lines out of asm/melee/mn/mndiagram.s into two new files: asm/melee/mn/mndiagram2.s and asm/melee/mn/mndiagram3.s.
Updated obj_files.mk with two added lines so the new mndiagram2 and mndiagram3 objects participate in the build.
Converted the code label lbl_8023F540 into a globally exported function symbol func_8023F540 in mndiagram.s.

The removed block from mndiagram.s begins around the region after lbl_802439E8 and includes functions such as func_80243A3C, func_80243A5C, func_80243A84, func_80243AB4, func_80243ADC, and func_80243BBC, indicating those routines were likely relocated to one of the new split files.
No functional intent beyond file splitting is documented in the provided PR text; the diff excerpt is dominated by assembly relocation.
smart_moves: Kept the split mechanical and paired it with the necessary obj_files.mk update, reducing the risk that moved assembly would silently stop being linked.
Promoted lbl_8023F540 to .global func_8023F540 at the split boundary, which is the kind of symbol visibility fix needed when a previously local label becomes referenced from another object file.
Used numbered continuation filenames mndiagram2.s and mndiagram3.s, preserving obvious association with the original mndiagram module while reducing single-file size.
The line counts strongly suggest most assembly bytes were preserved as-is rather than rewritten, which is appropriate for low-risk decomp staging work.
decomp_lessons: When splitting monolithic assembly files, audit all labels that become cross-file branch/call targets and promote them from local lbl_* labels to global func_* symbols when needed.
A mechanical assembly split still requires build-system changes; add every new split file to obj_files.mk in the correct order so addresses and link layout remain stable.
Large menu modules can be made easier to decompile incrementally by carving assembly into smaller object files before attempting C conversion.
Keep split PRs narrow: avoid mixing assembly relocation with semantic rewrites unless necessary, because preserving exact assembly makes matching regressions easier to diagnose.
The evidence here does not show higher-level C struct or header discoveries; this PR is primarily useful as a build/layout and symbol-boundary precedent.

naming_conventions.lesson: Numbered suffixes were used for continuation assembly files when splitting a large module without yet assigning more descriptive submodule names.
Labels at callable entry points that need cross-object visibility should use .global func_<address> naming rather than remaining anonymous/local lbl_<address> symbols.
Unidentified functions retained address-based func_* names during mechanical relocation; the PR did not introduce speculative semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mechanical relocation of contiguous assembly ranges into new .s files while preserving instruction text and address comments.
Explicit .global declaration inserted for func_8023F540 to satisfy link visibility after the file boundary changed.
Build-list update in obj_files.mk used alongside assembly file additions to maintain object generation.
The split appears to preserve address-based labels and function names, minimizing matching risk during reorganization.
No evidence in the slice of C matching tactics, register allocation tricks, or nonmatching-to-matching code changes.

review_feedback.detail: The provided PR context reports zero issue comments, zero review comments, and zero reviews, so no reviewer feedback can be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile asm/melee/mn/mndiagram2.s or asm/melee/mn/mndiagram3.s into C.
Check obj_files.mk around PR 233 to confirm the exact ordering of mndiagram.o, mndiagram2.o, and mndiagram3.o.
Search for func_8023F540 references after the split to understand why it needed to become global.
Look for later renames of func_80243A3C, func_80243ADC, and func_80243BBC to recover semantic names for the moved routines.
